Empires & Puzzles Cillian Review: The Undying 4-Star Slayer Strategy Guide

Remember Cillian? The 4-star Slayer with the 'shabby shoes' and the annoying habit of coming back to life? We revisit this self-resurrecting menace to see if his slow speed and aging stats can still cut it in the modern meta of Rush Tournaments and Bloody Battles.
Cillian: The Zombie Samurai That Just Won't Stay Dead
Meet Cillian, the "Undying" member of the

family. Released back in the ancient times of 2021, this 4-star Fire Fighter was designed to be a persistent nuisance. He's a slow-starting engine that, if left unchecked, becomes a self-healing, mana-generating machine. While he doesn't have a costume to freshen up his wardrobe or stats, his base kit offers a unique mix of damage and survivability that can still find a home in specific niches today.

Cillian sports a look that can best be described as "undead ronin meets European drifter." He’s got the dual-wielding stance of a samurai, the pale, cracked skin of someone who's seen the other side of the grave, and a red scarf that screams "anime protagonist." It's a gritty, dark aesthetic that fits the "Fell Shadows" theme perfectly. One community reviewer famously pointed out his "shitty shoes," proving that even when you're immortal, comfortable footwear is hard to find.
Let's address the elephant in the room: Cillian's stats are showing their age.

This is his signature move. If Cillian dies within

of casting this, he comes back with 27% health. This creates a "damned if you do, damned if you don't" scenario for the opponent. Do they kill him now and trigger the revive, or let him live and keep generating stacks?
is hilarious. He can revive from his special skill, and if that fails (or expires), he can revive from his talent grid. He is the cockroach of Empires & Puzzles—in the best way possible.
He needs to survive long enough to build these stacks. In modern fast-paced metas, this is his biggest hurdle.

Upon release, Cillian was a "4-Star All-Star." He was a staple in Rush Attack tournaments and a nightmare on defense because of the revive loop.
The game has gotten faster and hits harder. Waiting for Slow mana is a death sentence in many raids. His damage, once threatening, is now merely annoying.
. Set to Very Fast, he bypasses his slow start. The revive makes him a pain to clear from the tank or flank position.

. His revive brings him back with HP, which is one of the few ways to "heal" in this mode (since it's a resurrection, not a heal, though the mechanism is distinct). Plus, his fighter talent works here too.
You have two distinct ways to build Cillian, and both are valid depending on where you use him.
Maximizes his chance to survive until he casts his special. Increases the HP he has upon reviving (since 27% of a bigger pool is more HP).
His hit becomes negligible, serving only to refresh his revive buff.
Squeezes every ounce of damage out of his 315% hit. Since he revives anyway, you might as well make him hurt when he's alive.
Dies faster... but that's kind of his thing?
is recommended. If you are using Cillian in 2026, you likely need him to finish off enemies in Rush tournaments. A wet noodle hit won't help you there.
Combining his special skill with the Fighter class talent makes him incredibly sticky.
Once the Slayer stacks get rolling, the passive healing (outside of Bloody Battle) makes him hard to wear down.

The self-healing and mana stacks make him great for long, drawn-out boss fights in Tower events.
A wall of Fighters. Revive talents everywhere. Cillian protects himself with his own revive while Boldtusk keeps the team topped up.
Ensures the Family Bonus triggers 100% of the time, guaranteeing mana and health stacks every turn. This creates a snowball effect that is hard to stop if not dispelled immediately.

If you pull Cillian early, he is a fantastic carry for map stages. His self-healing saves you potions, and his revive saves you gems. Level him up, but prioritize his emblems only if you lack faster 4-star reds like
Cillian is a "fun" project, not a meta one. If you have excess Fighter emblems and want a nuisance for your 4-star Rush defense team, he's worth a look. Do
use Alpha Aethers on him unless you are absolutely dedicated to the Slayer aesthetic. There are far better targets for those precious resources.
The community has always had a soft spot for "The Undying."
One content creator described their defense team with Cillian as "Resurrection City," noting that between the Fighter talent and the Special Skill, Cillian just refuses to leave the battlefield.
A hilarious (if slightly crude) comment from the archives noted that Cillian "keeps resurrecting you" just like... well, you get the idea. He creates immense stamina for the team.
Early discussions focused heavily on the "Slow" speed being a dealbreaker, but the Family Bonus was quickly identified as the equalizer that made him playable.

Cillian is a relic from a time when "Slow" heroes were allowed to be powerful if they survived. In today's hyper-fast meta, he is often dead before he can cast his first spell. However, in the specific sandbox of
, the zombie samurai rises again. He is annoying, persistent, and satisfyingly dramatic when he clutches a win from the jaws of defeat.
Keep him for Rush wars and tournaments, but don't expect him to keep up with the modern power creep in standard play.
(Note: Cillian currently has no costume, so what you see is what you get!)
